display.purpose: Route heroes and high-level empty or onboarding moments.

headline.purpose: Page titles, drawer titles, and major panel headings.

body.purpose: Product copy, issue summaries, helper text, and explanatory content.

mono.purpose: Issue references, score deltas, API buckets, and repository metadata.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Visible keyboard focus and focused field state.

shadows.name: popover
shadows.purpose: Elevated API limits and compact overlays.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Current button, chip, row, and card transitions.

motion.name: drawer
motion.purpose: Current inspector drawer entrance and exit.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main local action such as search, save, or continue a workflow.
components.css: .ds-button-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; min-height: 36px; border: 1px solid rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--primary, #a78bfa); color: var(--on-primary, #0a0012); padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 200ms ease-in-out, border-color 200ms ease-in-out, transform 200ms ease-in-out; } .ds-button-primary:hover { border-color: rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.75); background: var(--primary-container, #7c3aed); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-button-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--primary, #a78bfa); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-button-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-button-primary:disabled { cursor: not-allowed; opacity: 0.45; transform: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action for refresh, inspect, cancel, and settings controls.
components.css: .ds-button-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; min-height: 36px; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 200ms ease-in-out, border-color 200ms ease-in-out, color 200ms ease-in-out, transform 200ms ease-in-out; } .ds-button-secondary:hover { border-color: var(--outline, #52525b); background: var(--surface-container-high, #18181b); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-button-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--primary, #a78bfa); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-button-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Accessible Icon Button
components.description: Canonical header bell/settings pattern. The button is named, and the decorative icon is hidden from assistive technology.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-button" type="button" aria-label="Review reminders"><svg class="ds-icon" aria-hidden="true" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M12 22a2.5 2.5 0 0 0 2.35-1.65h-4.7A2.5 2.5 0 0 0 12 22Zm7-6v-5a7 7 0 0 0-5-6.7V3a2 2 0 1 0-4 0v1.3A7 7 0 0 0 5 11v5l-2 2v1h18v-1l-2-2Zm-2 .5H7V11a5 5 0 0 1 10 0v5.5Z" fill="currentColor"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 32px; height: 32px; border: 0; border-radius: 9999px; background: transparent; color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); padding: 0; transition: background-color 160ms ease-out, color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-icon-button:hover { background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--primary, #a78bfa); } .ds-icon-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--primary, #a78bfa); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; flex: 0 0 auto; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Default search or settings text field on a dark product surface.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 6px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); } .ds-label { color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-input { min-height: 40px; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); padding: 10px 12px; font: inherit; font-size: 14px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--primary, #a78bfa); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.25); outline: none; }

components.name: Evidence Chip
components.description: Compact score, confidence, filter, or label token.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; white-space: nowrap; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); padding: 4px 12px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 200ms ease-in-out, border-color 200ms ease-in-out, color 200ms ease-in-out; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.5); background: var(--surface-container-high, #18181b); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); }

components.name: Workbench Card
components.description: Default card for metrics, issue summaries, settings panels, and inspector modules.
components.html:
Contribution Brief

Transparent fit evidence and next action guidance stay grouped in one surface.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); padding: 16px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.25; } .ds-card-copy { margin: 0; color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Navigation Tab
components.description: Sidebar or drawer route link with icon plus visible label.
components.html: Dashboard
components.css: .ds-nav-tab { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; border-radius: 8px; color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); padding: 12px 16px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 160ms ease-out, color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-tab:hover { background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); } .ds-nav-tab-active { background: var(--surface-container-high, #18181b); color: var(--primary, #a78bfa); } .ds-nav-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--primary, #a78bfa); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; flex: 0 0 auto; }

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Compact hover and focus-visible label for dense controls. It supplements accessible names and never replaces them.
components.css: .ds-tooltip-trigger { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 32px; height: 32px; border: 0; border-radius: 9999px; background: transparent; color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); } .ds-tooltip-trigger:hover, .ds-tooltip-trigger:focus-visible { color: var(--primary, #a78bfa); outline: none; } .ds-tooltip-trigger:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--primary, #a78bfa); } .ds-tooltip-icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; } .ds-tooltip { position: absolute; top: calc(100% + 8px); left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -2px); opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--surface-container-highest, #1e1e22); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); box-shadow: 0 12px 28px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.38); padding: 6px 8px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.2; white-space: nowrap; transition: opacity 120ms ease-out, transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-tooltip-trigger:hover .ds-tooltip, .ds-tooltip-trigger:focus-visible .ds-tooltip { opacity: 1; transform: translate(-50%, 0); }

components.name: Board Lane
components.description: Inset workflow lane for active or completed contribution cards.
components.css: .ds-board-lane { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; min-width: 0; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(9, 9, 11, 0.5); padding: 12px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-board-lane-header { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; } .ds-board-lane-header h3 { margin: 0; color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-board-lane-header span { color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); font-size: 12px; } .ds-board-card { display: grid; gap: 6px; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface-container, #121215); color: var(--on-surface, #fafafa); padding: 12px; overflow-wrap: anywhere; } .ds-board-card span { color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); font-size: 12px; }

components.name: Inspector Action Strip
components.description: Pinned action row below the inspector title that keeps primary issue actions available while reading evidence.
components.html: Action center
components.css: .ds-inspector-strip { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--outline-variant, #27272a); border-left: 0; border-right: 0; background: rgba(12, 12, 15, 0.95); padding: 16px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-strip-label { color: var(--on-surface-variant, #a1a1aa); font-size: 12px; } .ds-strip-actions { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; } .ds-inspector-strip .ds-button-primary, .ds-inspector-strip .ds-button-secondary { min-height: 34px; font-size: 12px; }

narrative.overview:
PR Dashboard is a dense product UI for contributors making practical decisions under uncertainty. It should feel like a focused workbench: dark, quiet, information-rich, and explicit about evidence. The interface earns trust by showing fit signals, risks, local workflow state, and privacy boundaries without turning the experience into a tutorial.

The current visual system is a high-contrast dark product surface built from near-black layers, thin borders, Geist typography, violet primary actions, emerald success states, and red errors. This file documents that current system so future agents stay aligned while making changes. It is not a redesign pass.

The system must not become a GitHub visual replica, and it must not look like a generic Shadcn/Vercel observability tool. The current Zinc-on-Violet dark-mode dev aesthetic is a known trap. Future refinement should preserve the workflow clarity while giving PR Dashboard a more specific contributor-decision identity.
keyCharacteristics: Dense product surfaces for scanning, comparison, and repeated action.
Flat-by-default layering with borders and tonal surfaces instead of decorative depth.
Restrained semantic color: violet for action and selection, emerald for positive state, red for error or pass risk.
Copy that is precise, candid, and useful.
Local-first trust patterns that keep token and storage boundaries visible.

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Color must explain state, action, or evidence. Do not use violet, emerald, or red as decoration.

rules.name: The Trap Naming Rule
rules.body: Do not let "dark dev tool" become the design rationale. The Zinc-on-Violet palette is the current implementation, not the product identity.

rules.name: The Product Type Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce display fonts into controls, labels, cards, or data-heavy surfaces. Product legibility wins over typographic novelty.

rules.name: The Candid Copy Rule
rules.body: Favor concrete product language such as "Local Session Storage by Default", "no private scopes needed", and "Save candidates from Find Contributions to see them on your Dashboard." Avoid hype, magic, and vague motivation.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: Use tonal layers and 1px borders for most hierarchy. Shadows are for overlays, focus, and true spatial overlap.

rules.name: The No Fake Glass Rule
rules.body: Backdrop blur and glass-like treatment must be rare and purposeful. Do not use glassmorphism as a default card or panel style.

dos: Do document and preserve local-first trust boundaries. Token, export/import, Proof Log, and browser storage copy must stay concrete.
Do make score, confidence, risk, and next action visible enough for users to trust or challenge the recommendation.
Do use standard product affordances for navigation, filters, forms, drawers, cards, popovers, and buttons.
Do use semantic color for evidence, action, warning, success, and error.
Do give icon-only controls accessible names and hide decorative icons from assistive technology.
Do keep dense screens scannable through consistent spacing, clear grouping, and low-noise labels.
donts: Don't become a GitHub visual replica.
Don't look like a generic Shadcn/Vercel observability tool.
Don't treat the Zinc-on-Violet dark-mode dev aesthetic as the goal.
Don't gamify contribution work with streaks, badges, confetti, leaderboards, or point-like reward language.
Don't imply backend trust guarantees, live sync, OAuth, encrypted sync, or remote merge verification that v1 does not provide.
Don't use glassmorphism, gradient text, side-stripe borders, identical card grids, or hero-metric templates as default patterns.
Don't rely on color alone for score, confidence, risk, success, warning, or error states.
